name: GrowthBook MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official GrowthBook MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with GrowthBook's experimentation and feature flag platform — managing A/B experiments, querying experiment results, creating feature flags, and integrating GrowthBook's open-source experimentation engine into agent-driven product analytics and release management workflows.
use_cases: Creating and managing A/B experiments from product analytics agents
Querying experiment results and statistical significance from analysis agents
Managing feature flags for progressive rollouts from deployment agents
Querying feature flag states from release management agents
Analyzing experiment performance data from data science agents
Coordinating product experiments with data warehouse metrics from BI agents
not_for: Teams using Optimizely, LaunchDarkly, or Statsig as primary experimentation platform
Simple feature flags without experimentation needs (simpler tools exist)
Teams without data warehouse connectivity for experiment analysis
best_when: An agent needs to create, manage, or analyze GrowthBook A/B experiments and feature flags — especially integrated with data warehouse metrics.
avoid_when: You're using Optimizely, LaunchDarkly, or another A/B testing platform.

auth.notes: GrowthBook personal access tokens with role-based scopes. Organization-level tokens for admin operations. Read-only and read-write scopes available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: fully free, self-hosted; Cloud: free up to 3 users
pricing.notes: GrowthBook is open source and free to self-host. Cloud offering has generous free tier. Enterprise adds SSO, advanced permissions, and dedicated support. MCP server is official open source.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits apply; self-hosted configurable

known_agent_gotchas: GrowthBook requires data warehouse integration for statistical analysis — not standalone
Experiment results require time to accumulate — agents must check if results are ready
Feature flag targeting conditions use GrowthBook's attribute system — must match SDK context
Self-hosted vs Cloud have same API but different base URLs
Experiment phases must be advanced manually or via schedule — not automatic
Organization ID required for all API calls
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (Cloud). Role-based access tokens with read/write scopes. SOC 2, GDPR. Official GrowthBook MCP server. Self-hosted TLS is operator responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (Cloud). Role-based tokens with read/write scopes. SOC 2, GDPR. Official GrowthBook MCP server.
